sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The Rule of Nines is a rapid method for estimating the total body surface area (TBSA) affected by burns in adults. It is useful for early burn-size estimation and guiding escalation and fluid-resuscitation decisions.

- Adult Rule of Nines:
- Head and neck 9%
- Each arm 9%
- Anterior trunk 18%
- Posterior trunk 18%
- Each leg 18%
- Perineum 1%
- For children, the Rule of Nines is less accurate; use a paediatric chart such as Lund and Browder where available
